evals.prompt: Our B2B SaaS has 12% monthly churn. Our biggest segment is SMBs at $99/month. Usage analytics show that customers who churn use the product 60% less in weeks 2-3 compared to those who stay. What churn prevention interventions should we run?
evals.expected_output: Churn intervention strategy using the usage signal, including: trigger-based intervention design, messaging templates, CS escalation process, and success metrics.
assertions: Uses the weeks 2-3 usage drop as the key early warning signal
Designs a specific intervention triggered by the usage decline
Provides messaging approach for the intervention (email, in-app, CS call)
Addresses root cause investigation (why does usage drop?)
Sets success metrics for the intervention program

evals.prompt: We have 200 churned customers from the last 6 months. How do we run a win-back campaign?
evals.expected_output: Win-back campaign strategy with segmentation (recency, reason for churn, plan type), messaging sequence, offer design, and expected conversion rate benchmarks.
assertions: Recommends segmenting churned customers by recency and reason
Covers win-back offer design (discount, feature update, new value prop)
Provides a multi-touch sequence (not just one email)
Sets realistic conversion rate expectations (2-5% is typical for win-back)
Addresses how to identify churned customers most likely to return

evals.prompt: Write a cancellation email for when customers want to cancel
evals.expected_output: Provides churn prevention strategy for the cancellation moment (save offers, exit survey, pause option), and redirects to copywriting or email-sequence skill for the actual email writing.
assertions: Covers the cancellation moment strategy (save offer, pause option, exit survey)
Notes the email writing itself is better handled by copywriting skill
Recommends capturing cancellation reason as a data point
